expectations.text: Recognizes kickoff, walks SKN-OPS-003 rather than auto-firing
expectations.evidence: Treats this as a serious situation and doesn't execute anything, but never cites SKN-OPS-003 or its structure; some lines read as directive ('Immediate ask: hold...') rather than a staged SOP walk.
No phase structure; jumps directly to drafting internal/retailer/customer notices in the same pass as the signal summary.

expectations.text: Drafts customer notification in multiple forms
expectations.evidence: Internal notice, retailer hold notice, and customer notice are all drafted (draft only, not sent).

expectations.text: Drafts §4.D return/disposal plan with oc3pl-order-manager handoff
expectations.evidence: No disposal or return-logistics plan; no oc3pl-order-manager handoff named.
CAPA and capa-coordinator are never mentioned.

expectations.text: Names cross-functional asks with owners + explicit gates, no comms sent
expectations.evidence: Ends with open questions and confirms nothing was sent or executed, though not a structured owner table.

eval_feedback.overall: Critically, baseline never checked whether 'lot 24-11' actually exists in PLM or the Complaint Log — it accepted the batch number at face value. The with-skill run caught that this batch number doesn't match any real record. That's a meaningful miss on a recall workflow specifically, where acting on an unverified batch number is a real risk.
